steps.action: Set viewport to 768px width, navigate to /kanvas?studio=worldview
steps.expected: Page renders correctly with no horizontal overflow
steps.observed: Page renders correctly. document.body.scrollWidth (768) === document.body.clientWidth (768). No horizontal scrollbar.

steps.action: Check body overflow dimensions via JS eval at 768px
Create 10 scenes to overflow scene strip, verify horizontal scroll within strip container
steps.expected: Scene strip scrolls horizontally within its container without causing body overflow
steps.observed: Scene strip container: scrollWidth=1350 > clientWidth=696 (overflow-x-auto working). Body remains scrollWidth=768 === clientWidth=768. No body overflow.

steps.action: Seed takes via store (addTake with sceneId) and set viewport to 600px
steps.expected: Takes grid switches to 2 columns below 640px (sm breakpoint)
steps.observed: Takes grid has classes 'grid grid-cols-2 gap-3 sm:grid-cols-3'. Computed gridTemplateColumns='270px 270px' (2 columns). 3 take cards display in 2+1 layout.

steps.action: Check console errors throughout all tests

evidence.network: N/A (no API calls in this test)

frictions.description: The addTake store method requires a sceneId field on the take object, not a separate sceneId parameter as suggested in the task prompt. The task prompt provided addTake(sceneId, takeObj) but the actual API is addTake({ sceneId, ...takeObj }).
frictions.resolution: Inspected store source code, corrected the addTake call to include sceneId in the take object.

summary: Tested 1 assertion (VAL-POLISH-005): PASSED. The fix in commit 8acaf80 (adding min-w-0 to flex-1 content container) successfully resolves the horizontal overflow at 768px viewport. At 768px: body scrollWidth === clientWidth (768). Scene strip scrolls horizontally within its container (scrollWidth 1350 > clientWidth 696) without causing body overflow. At 600px: takes grid correctly uses 2 columns (grid-cols-2 with computed gridTemplateColumns='270px 270px'). No console errors detected.
